source: All-In Podcast — 2025 predictions (bestie guest Gavin Baker)
summary: FSD is crossing into mainstream adoption and Tesla's autonomous driving will compound at an accelerating rate in 2025
summary_extended: Baker argues that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology has crossed a functional threshold and is already influencing real-world consumer behavior, citing personal anecdotes such as preferring Tesla-based Uber rides at night as evidence that FSD 'works today.' He believes 2025 will be the year FSD transitions from early-adopter curiosity to mainstream adoption, with the rate of adoption compounding as more consumers experience the technology firsthand. The investment implication is straightforwardly bullish on Tesla, as accelerating FSD adoption would drive both hardware sales and high-margin software/subscription revenue. Baker does not provide specific price targets, penetration rates, or revenue figures, making this a qualitative conviction call rather than a quantitatively grounded thesis.
detail: Baker states FSD 'works today' and is already influencing consumer behavior (preferring Tesla Uber rides at night). He expects mainstream adoption to accelerate in 2025.
confidence_evidence: Baker expresses personal conviction but does not provide quantitative targets or deep structural analysis
quote_fragment: FSD works today and it's going to cross into mainstream adoption

ticker_audit.reasoning: The thesis is specifically and exclusively about Tesla's FSD product reaching mainstream adoption in 2025; Tesla is the direct subject of the investment claim.
